content: Pediatric pharmacology differs from adults due to developmental changes in ADME processes. Neonates/infants: immature hepatic enzymes (reduced glucuronidation - chloramphenicol gray baby syndrome), reduced GFR (aminoglycoside toxicity risk), higher total body water (increased Vd for hydrophilic drugs), lower protein binding (increased free drug). Children (1-12 years): often faster metabolism than adults (higher mg/kg doses needed for some drugs), mature renal function by 6-12 months. Adolescents: approaching adult physiology but hormonal changes affect drug response. Body composition changes: premature neonates 85% water, adults 60%. Gastric pH higher at birth (affects absorption of weak acids/bases); gastric emptying slower in neonates. Blood-brain barrier more permeable in infants.
